evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Count to my .NET for iOS app. Here's my empty view controller: EmptyViewController.cs. I need to count EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes, show the built-in counting UI, and when the user taps the List button store the scanned barcodes so I can show them on a results screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete BarcodeCount integration code into EmptyViewController.cs: DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey, a manually-managed Camera (Camera.GetDefaultCamera() + camera.ApplySettingsAsync(BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings) + dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync), new BarcodeCountSettings() enabling only Ean13Upca and Code128, BarcodeCount.Create(context, settings), BarcodeCountView.Create(View.Bounds, context, barcodeCount[, style]) added via View.AddSubview, barcodeCount.Scanned event (or AddListener) that copies session.RecognizedBarcodes out via InvokeOnMainThread, ListButtonTapped handler storing the barcodes, camera SwitchToDesiredStateAsync On/Standby + barcodeCount.Enabled across ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear. Shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription, AppDelegate initialize for SDK 8.0+, SupportedOSPlatformVersion 15.0, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions calling ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() in AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching (required for SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions setting SupportedOSPlatformVersion to at least 15.0
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new BarcodeCountSettings() is used (the settings use a plain constructor)
BarcodeCount.Create( is used as the factory (NOT new BarcodeCount(...), which has no public constructor)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
Native-style symbology names (e.g. Symbology.ean13UPCA, .code128) are NOT used
A Camera is obtained via Camera.GetDefaultCamera() (or Camera.DefaultCamera) and BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings is applied via camera.ApplySettingsAsync(...)
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera (the camera is managed explicitly, not by the view)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) and FrameSourceState.Standby (or .Off) are used to drive the camera across the lifecycle
barcodeCount.Enabled is set to true so frames are processed
BarcodeCountView.Create( is called (not new BarcodeCountView(...), which is not a public constructor)
BarcodeCountView.Create( receives a CGRect frame (e.g. this.View.Bounds) as its first argument (NOT an Android Context)
The BarcodeCountView is added to the view hierarchy via this.View.AddSubview(...) (the view IS a UIView)
Camera lifecycle is driven from ViewWillAppear / ViewWillDisappear (iOS view controller lifecycle), not Android OnResume/OnPause
No barcodeCountView.OnResume() / OnPause() / Start() / Stop() calls are made (those do not exist on BarcodeCountView)
Either barcodeCount.Scanned += handler or barcodeCount.AddListener(IBarcodeCountListener) is wired
The scan handler reads args.Session.RecognizedBarcodes (the BarcodeCountSession exposes RecognizedBarcodes, not AddedTrackedBarcodes)

The recognized barcodes are copied out of the session (e.g. .ToList()) inside the callback, and UI work is dispatched onto the main thread via UIApplication.SharedApplication.InvokeOnMainThread (or DispatchQueue.MainQueue), NOT Android's RunOnUiThread
A barcodeCountView.ListButtonTapped handler is wired to store/show the scanned barcodes
No Android-only APIs are used (no Android Context, no AddView, no RunOnUiThread, no AppCompatActivity, no AndroidManifest)
If the listener interface is used, OnObservationStarted and OnObservationStopped are implemented (IBarcodeCountListener has three methods)
No BarcodeArView, IBarcodeArHighlightProvider, or BarcodeAr API is used (MatrixScan Count is not MatrixScan AR)
No DataCaptureView or BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created (BarcodeCountView is the counting UI surface)
No MAUI XAML or Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui package is introduced (this is a non-MAUI iOS app)

evals.prompt: I'm building a receiving app with MatrixScan Count on .NET iOS. I already have it counting (see IntegratedViewController.cs). Now I want to check scanned barcodes against an expected list: 3x of 0123456789012 and 1x of 9876543210987, and react when everything on the list has been scanned.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a capture list: builds TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) entries, creates a BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets), implements IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener (OnObservationStarted/OnObservationStopped/OnCaptureListSessionUpdated/OnCaptureListCompleted), and applies it via barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list), preserving existing integration.

assertions.text: TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) is used to build the expected barcodes (factory, not new TargetBarcode(...))
A TargetBarcode for 0123456789012 with quantity 3 and one for 9876543210987 with quantity 1 are created
BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets) is used (factory, not new BarcodeCountCaptureList(...))
IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener is implemented with OnObservationStarted, OnObservationStopped, OnCaptureListSessionUpdated, and OnCaptureListCompleted
barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list) is called to apply the list
OnCaptureListCompleted is where the all-scanned reaction is handled
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler, ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app — silent counting. Update the feedback in IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCount.Feedback to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor = silent), imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback, preserving the rest of the integration. It does NOT call DefaultFeedback as a method.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.Feedback is reassigned to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor)
BarcodeCountFeedback is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback
BarcodeCountFeedback.DefaultFeedback is NOT called as a method (with parentheses) — it is a static property
The feedback slots are referred to as Success / Failure if mentioned (NOT Scanned / Tapped, which are MatrixScan AR's)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved
